title: Sour Crunchy German Potato Hash
description: A plant-based German dish featuring crispy potatoes, sour flavors, and a hint of sweetness from condensed milk, perfect for a lazy Sunday. This stir-fry is a unique blend of textures and tastes.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced potatoes and cook until they start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onions and bell peppers to the skillet and continue to cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the condensed milk, apple cider vinegar, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the milk mixture over the potatoes and vegetables in the skillet and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer the mixture for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Increase the heat to medium-high and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the potatoes are crispy and golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the heat and garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the Sour Crunchy German Potato Hash hot, straight from the skillet.
Enjoy as a lazy Sunday breakfast or brunch dish.
